metadata.source: CITC + NDMO + SAMA + MCIT + NCA + SDAIA + ZATCA
metadata.source_ar: هيئة الاتصالات وتقنية المعلومات + المكتب الوطني لإدارة البيانات + البنك المركزي السعودي + وزارة الاتصالات + الهيئة الوطنية للأمن السيبراني + الهيئة السعودية للبيانات والذكاء الاصطناعي + هيئة الزكاة والضريبة والجمارك
metadata.notes: Saudi Arabia technology sector regulations covering ICT licensing, data protection, fintech, e-commerce, and cybersecurity as of Q1 2025.

citc_license_types.notes: CITC regulates all ICT service providers in Saudi Arabia. Licenses are categorized by service type. All applicants must be Saudi-registered entities.

requirements: Saudi-registered company with minimum 30% Saudi ownership
Minimum capital of SAR 10,000,000
Technical infrastructure plan approved by CITC
Network security and lawful interception capability
Content filtering compliance per CITC regulations
items.notes_ar: يتطلب بنية تحتية للشبكة وقدرات أمنية معتمدة من الهيئة
items.notes_en: Requires approved network infrastructure, content filtering, and lawful interception capabilities.

requirements: Saudi-registered company
Partnership agreement with licensed telecom operator
Content compliance with CITC standards
Consumer protection mechanisms in place
items.notes_ar: تشمل خدمات الرسائل القصيرة والمحتوى الرقمي والتطبيقات
items.notes_en: Covers SMS services, digital content, mobile apps, and other value-added telecom services.

items.regulator_ar: هيئة الاتصالات وتقنية المعلومات + المكتب الوطني لإدارة البيانات
requirements: Saudi-registered entity or licensed foreign branch
Data center presence in Saudi Arabia (or commitment to establish within 12 months)
NDMO Cloud Service Provider certification
Compliance with Cloud Computing Regulatory Framework (CCRF)
Data residency guarantees for government and sensitive data
required_documents: Business continuity and disaster recovery plan
items.notes_ar: يجب الحصول على شهادة المكتب الوطني لإدارة البيانات لخدمة الجهات الحكومية
items.notes_en: NDMO CSP certification is mandatory to serve government entities. Local data center presence required.

requirements: Minimum Tier III data center classification (Uptime Institute or equivalent)
Physical security and access controls per NCA standards
Redundant power and cooling systems
Saudi-registered entity with minimum capital of SAR 5,000,000
Fire suppression and environmental monitoring systems
items.notes_ar: يجب أن يكون مركز البيانات داخل المملكة ويستوفي معايير التصنيف الدولية
items.notes_en: Data center must be located within Saudi Arabia and meet international tier classification standards.

items.regulator_ar: الهيئة الوطنية للأمن السيبراني + هيئة الاتصالات
requirements: NCA-registered cybersecurity service provider
Certified cybersecurity professionals on staff (minimum 3)
Compliance with NCA Cybersecurity Service Provider requirements
Saudi-registered entity
Professional liability insurance
required_documents: Staff certifications (CISSP, CISM, CEH, or equivalent)
items.notes_ar: يجب التسجيل لدى الهيئة الوطنية للأمن السيبراني وتوظيف كوادر معتمدة
items.notes_en: Must register with NCA and employ certified cybersecurity professionals.

requirements: CITC IoT regulatory framework compliance
Device certification for radio frequency compliance
Data privacy and security measures per PDPL
Saudi-registered entity
Spectrum usage authorization (if applicable)
items.notes_ar: تشمل الأجهزة المتصلة والمنصات الذكية ويجب الامتثال لتنظيمات الطيف الترددي
items.notes_en: Covers connected devices and smart platforms. RF spectrum compliance required for wireless IoT devices.

requirements: Commercial Registration with e-commerce activity code
Maroof platform registration
Compliance with E-Commerce Law (Royal Decree M/126)
Consumer protection and return policy publication
Payment gateway integration with Mada support
ZATCA e-invoicing (Fatoorah) compliance
items.notes_ar: يجب التسجيل في منصة معروف والامتثال لنظام التجارة الإلكترونية
items.notes_en: Must register on Maroof platform and comply with Saudi E-Commerce Law. Mada payment support mandatory.

requirements: Innovative fintech solution not currently available in Saudi market
Saudi-registered company or willingness to establish one
Minimum capital as determined by SAMA based on activity type
AML/CFT compliance framework
Consumer protection mechanisms
Technology risk management plan
required_documents: Proof of concept or prototype demonstration
items.notes_ar: مخصص للحلول المالية المبتكرة. مدة الصندوق التجريبي من 12 إلى 24 شهراً
items.notes_en: For innovative financial solutions. Sandbox period is 12-24 months with possibility of full license upon graduation.

requirements: Commercial Registration with IT services activity code
PDPL compliance for data processing
Service level agreement templates
Data hosting disclosure (local or international)
items.notes_ar: تسجيل تجاري مع نشاط تقنية المعلومات. لا تتطلب ترخيصاً خاصاً من الهيئة إلا إذا تضمنت بيانات حكومية
items.notes_en: Requires CR with IT activity code. Special CITC/NDMO licensing only if handling government data.

requirements: Authorized reseller agreement with licensed telecom operator
Saudi-registered entity
CITC reseller registration
Consumer complaint handling process
Compliance with CITC pricing and billing regulations
items.notes_ar: يتطلب اتفاقية مع مشغل اتصالات مرخص من الهيئة
items.notes_en: Requires formal authorization agreement with a CITC-licensed telecom operator.

ndmo_data_localization.notes: NDMO data classification and localization requirements apply to all government entities and their contractors. Private sector encouraged to adopt.

classifications.description_en: Data intended for public disclosure with no restrictions
Data for internal organizational use, not intended for public release
Data whose disclosure could harm organizational or national interests
classifications.cross_border_transfer: Requires NDMO approval and adequate safeguards
classifications.cloud_requirements: NDMO-certified CSP with local data center required

classifications.description_en: Data whose disclosure could cause significant damage to national security or critical interests
classifications.cross_border_transfer: Prohibited. Must remain within Saudi borders
classifications.cloud_requirements: NDMO-certified CSP with sovereign cloud infrastructure in Saudi Arabia. Government Community Cloud preferred

csp_certification_requirements: Data center located within Saudi Arabia
Compliance with NDMO Cloud First Policy
Business continuity plan with local failover

sama_fintech_sandbox.notes: SAMA operates the Fintech Regulatory Sandbox to enable innovative financial services in a controlled environment.
application_requirements: Innovative solution addressing a gap in the Saudi financial market
Functional prototype or proof of concept
Clear value proposition for Saudi consumers or businesses
Risk mitigation and consumer protection strategy
Exit strategy in case of sandbox failure

sandbox_phases.description: Initial application review, due diligence, and eligibility assessment by SAMA
Controlled testing with limited customers (max 500), capped transaction volumes
Expanded customer base (up to 5,000), increased transaction limits, performance monitoring
Assessment for full licensing or orderly wind-down of operations

capital_requirements_sar.notes: Capital requirements may be reduced during sandbox phase at SAMA discretion. Full capital required upon graduation.

graduation_criteria: Successful completion of all sandbox phases
No material regulatory breaches during testing
Demonstrated consumer protection and complaint handling
Sustainable business model with clear path to profitability

ecommerce_regulations.regulator_ar: وزارة التجارة + هيئة الاتصالات + هيئة الزكاة والضريبة والجمارك

requirements.description: E-commerce stores must disclose: business name, CR number, contact details, physical address, and Maroof registration
Consumers have the right to return products within 7 days of receipt if unused and in original packaging. Digital products excluded unless defective. Full refund within 14 days of return approval
All e-commerce platforms must provide terms and conditions in Arabic. Bilingual is acceptable but Arabic is legally binding
All e-commerce platforms operating in Saudi Arabia must support Mada debit card payments. Additional payment methods (Visa, Mastercard, Apple Pay, STC Pay) are optional
All taxable businesses must generate e-invoices in ZATCA-approved format. Phase 1 (Generation) effective Dec 2021. Phase 2 (Integration) rolled out in waves based on revenue thresholds
E-commerce platforms must obtain explicit consent before collecting personal data, provide clear privacy policy, and allow consumers to request data deletion per PDPL

penalties.notes: Penalties may include temporary or permanent store closure in addition to fines

pdpl_compliance.regulator: SDAIA - Saudi Data and Artificial Intelligence Authority
pdpl_compliance.law_reference: Royal Decree M/19 - Personal Data Protection Law (effective September 2023, enforcement from September 2024)

key_requirements.description: Explicit consent required before collecting or processing personal data. Consent must be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous. Exceptions for legal obligations and vital interests.
Data controllers must notify SDAIA within 72 hours of discovering a personal data breach. Affected individuals must be notified without undue delay if breach poses high risk to their rights.
Organizations processing large-scale personal data or sensitive data must appoint a DPO. Threshold: processing data of more than 100,000 individuals or systematic monitoring activities.
Individuals have the right to: access their data, request correction, request deletion, restrict processing, data portability, and object to automated decision-making.
Transfer of personal data outside Saudi Arabia requires adequate level of protection in receiving country (as determined by SDAIA) or binding corporate rules and contractual safeguards.

penalties.notes: Penalties can include both fines and imprisonment. SDAIA may order data destruction or processing suspension.

nca_cybersecurity.notes: NCA cybersecurity controls are mandatory for government entities, critical national infrastructure operators, and their suppliers.

frameworks.applicable_to: All government entities and critical infrastructure operators
Operators of critical national infrastructure (energy, water, telecom, finance, health, transport)
Cloud service providers and cloud consumers in government and critical sectors

assessment_types.description: Organizations conduct internal assessment against NCA controls. Required annually.
Assessment by NCA-authorized cybersecurity assessment providers. Required for critical infrastructure every 2 years.
NCA may conduct direct assessments at any time for critical entities or in response to incidents.

non_compliance_consequences: Warning and corrective action plan requirement
Escalation to entity leadership and relevant minister
Potential service suspension for critical non-compliance
